2026-02-12 20:02:18.230581+00:00;16458;model;Here is a fully operational implementation using the **SHADE (Success-History based Adaptive Differential Evolution) with Restarts** algorithm.

SHADE is an advanced variant of Differential Evolution that is widely considered state-of-the-art for continuous optimization. It outperforms standard DE and jDE by automatically adapting control parameters ($F$ and $CR$) using a historical memory of successful updates
column2: and by using an external archive to maintain diversity.

column1: 1.  **Historical Memory**: The algorithm maintains a memory of $F$ (scaling factor) and $CR$ (crossover rate) values that historically led to improvements. New parameters are sampled from distributions based on this memory
column2: biasing the search towards successful strategies.

column1: 2.  **Current-to-pbest Mutation**: Instead of mutating towards a random individual
column2: agents mutate towards one of the top $p\%$ best individuals ($p$-best). This accelerates convergence.

column1: 3.  **External Archive**: Inferior solutions replaced during selection are moved to an archive. The difference vector ($x_{r1} - x_{r2}$) can draw $x_{r2}$ from this archive
column2: significantly increasing the diversity of potential search directions.

column1: 4.  **Restart Strategy**: If the population converges (variance drops) or the search stagnates
column2: the algorithm restarts with a fresh population (keeping the global best found so far) to explore new areas of the search space within the remaining time.

column1: Minimizes a function using SHADE (Success-History Adaptive DE) with Restarts.
# Use 98% of the time budget to ensure we return before the external timeout
# Larger populations explore better but are slower per generation.
# Memory for Adaptive Parameters (F and CR)
k_mem = 0 # Pointer to the current memory slot to update
# Elitism: Inject global best solution if we have one (from previous restart)
start_eval_idx = 1 # Skip re-evaluating the injected best
# Assign a random memory index to each individual
# Generate CR: Normal distribution around memory
# Generate F: Cauchy distribution around memory

column2: retry (simple heuristic: up to 5 times then fallback)

column1: # Sort population to find top p-best individuals
# p_best rate controls greediness (0.11 is a standard robust value)
# Select pbest for each individual randomly from top %
# Select r1: Random distinct from current i
# Select r2: Random distinct from i and r1
# Handle collisions: r2 != i and r2 != r1
# Note: i and r1 are always < pop_size. r2 can be >= pop_size (archive).
# Compute Mutant Vector: v = x + F*(pbest - x) + F*(r1 - r2)
mutant = pop + f_col * (x_pbest - pop) + f_col * (x_r1 - x_r2)
# Force at least one dimension from mutant
# Check time strictly before expensive eval
# Add parent to archive before replacement
# Update Memory (Smooth update to be robust)
# If population fitness variance is negligible
